methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $142M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
Lumafield is a Series C robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $142M to date. The valuation analysis draws on two primary methods applied against a comparable set of nine same-sector, same/adjacent-stage peers, including both private companies (Hadrian at $1.6B Series C, 1X Technologies at $10.0B Series B, Exotec at $2.0B Series D, Bedrock Robotics at $1.8B Series B, and Dexterity at $1.6B Series B) and one public comparable.

Stage-progression methodology, applying a typical 1.7x multiplier to the $142M capital raised proxy, yields $241M. The peer-median approach across the nine-company comparable set, which spans $300M to $10.0B in valuation range, implies $1.6B. The peer set skews toward higher valuations at the frontier of robotics venture capital, with a median substantially elevated from early-stage benchmarks.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $205M (low) to $1.8B (high), with a median of $921M. The wide spread reflects material dispersion in the peer set and methodological tension between capital-raised-based approaches and market-implied multiples for comparable stages. Key uncertainties include stage-specific valuation step-up assumptions and whether Lumafield's specific product positioning and traction align with peers at the median or upper quartile of the distribution.
